financial.revenue: Q4 FY26 revenue ₹617.22 crore, roughly flat vs ₹624.81 crore YoY; FY26 full year record ₹2,153.69 crore, up 11.34% YoY
financial.growth: FY26 full-year revenue growth 11.34% YoY; no specific FY27 revenue growth target disclosed
financial.margins: FY26 EBITDA margin 16.50% (down from 18.93% in FY25); Q4 FY26 operating profit ₹79.77 crore, down 30% YoY due to one-time Labour Code provisions and rising input costs
financial.capex: ₹25 crore approved for new land near Vadodara, funded through internal accruals; new Power Transformer factory under construction, expected commissioning July 2026

business.expansion: New Power Transformer manufacturing facility under construction, expected to be commissioned in July 2026
business.products: Dry-type industrial transformers and distribution transformers for cement, steel, refinery, chemical, and mining sectors; also power transformers
business.geography: Domestic core industrial sector plus exports to Gulf and Southeast Asia markets

commitments: Commission new power transformer facility by July 2026
₹25 crore investment in Vadodara land for future expansion, funded via internal accruals
risks: One-time Labour Code provisions and rising input costs pressuring Q4 margins - Medium
Negative mark-to-market gains impacting Q4 EBITDA - Medium
FY26 EBITDA margin declined ~243 bps YoY (18.93% to 16.50%) - Medium

guidance.summary: FY26 record revenue of ₹2,153.69 crore (+11.34% YoY) was accompanied by a 6.2% decline in net profit to ₹305.38 crore, with stock falling ~19% post-results

deal_wins: New orders worth ₹310 crore (2,107 MVA) added in April 2026, no specific client type disclosed

order_book.total_value: Order backlog of ~₹1,200 crore (10,270 MVA) entering FY27

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Enquiry pipeline described as strong; no specific ₹ value or conversion rate disclosed

customers.net_assessment: Not disclosed; company takes a 'selective approach to order intake' per management commentary

revenue_by_segment.Dry-type/Distribution transformers: Core business; specific % revenue split not disclosed
revenue_by_segment.Power transformers: New facility under construction, expected commissioning July 2026; not yet contributing to revenue split
revenue_by_segment.Exports (Gulf, Southeast Asia): Contributed positively to Q4 revenue; specific % not disclosed

segments.segment_outlook: Power transformer segment expected to accelerate post-July 2026 commissioning; core dry-type/distribution segment facing margin pressure from input costs

geography.expansion_plans: Export market development in Gulf and Southeast Asia continuing per FY27 outlook commentary; no specific new countries/timeline disclosed

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Rising input costs flagged as a key Q4 margin pressure factor, intensifying

capex_detailed.total: ₹25 crore approved for land acquisition near Vadodara; additional capex for new power transformer facility amount not separately disclosed
capex_detailed.facility_expansion: New power transformer manufacturing facility, expected commissioning July 2026; ₹25 crore Vadodara land acquisition for future expansion

debt_management.current_net_debt: Not disclosed - company appears net cash given dividend payout of ₹100/share (1000%) and internal-accrual funding

market_share.by_segment: Not disclosed with specific share figures
market_share.overall_trend: Mixed - record revenue but margin compression and declining net profit suggest competitive/cost pressures

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Not explicitly disclosed; selective order intake approach suggests some pricing discipline amid rising input costs
competitive_advantages: Specialisation in dry-type transformers for hazardous environments (cement, steel, refinery, mining) commanding premium positioning
Strong order backlog of ~₹1,200 crore providing revenue visibility into FY27
Export diversification into Gulf and Southeast Asia markets
competitive_threats: Rising input costs and one-time Labour Code provisions compressing margins - Medium-High
EBITDA margin decline of ~243 bps YoY (18.93% to 16.50%) in FY26 - Medium
Negative mark-to-market gains affecting Q4 profitability - Low-Medium

guidance.investor_verdict: Despite record FY26 revenue of ₹2,153.69 crore (+11.34%), a 6.2% YoY decline in net profit and margin compression to 16.50% EBITDA margin triggered a ~19% stock price drop post-results

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Stock fell ~19% to ₹10,100 on results day; analyst targets range ₹8,000-10,500, with current trailing P/E of 32.75 and EV/EBITDA of 23.34 reflecting a valuation reset post weak Q4
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 operating profit declined 30% YoY to ₹79.77 crore against a backdrop of record annual revenue, indicating results likely missed margin expectations despite top-line resilience
analyst_dimensions.achievability: FY27 outlook described as 'constructive' with industrial capex cycle continuing; order backlog of ₹1,200 crore plus April 2026 fresh orders of ₹310 crore support near-term revenue visibility
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: FY26 margin decline driven by one-time Labour Code provisions, rising input costs, and negative mark-to-market gains - partly one-off (Labour Code) but input cost pressure could persist
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: ₹25 crore Vadodara land purchase and new power transformer facility (commissioning July 2026) funded entirely via internal accruals; specific ROIC not disclosed
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: Order backlog of ~₹1,200 crore (10,270 MVA) at FY27 start, plus ₹310 crore (2,107 MVA) added in April 2026; execution timeline not disclosed, but selective order intake suggests quality over volume
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunity: new power transformer facility commissioning July 2026 to diversify product mix and capture India capex cycle (Medium probability, High impact). Risk: persistent input cost inflation and margin compression (Medium-High probability, Medium impact)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Management transparently disclosed the specific drivers of Q4 margin pressure (Labour Code provisions, input costs, MTM losses) rather than masking the profit decline, suggesting transparent reporting
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: 6.2% FY26 net profit decline (EPS ₹301.85 vs ₹321.65 in FY25) likely pressures near-term FY27 EPS estimates downward despite revenue growth, pending margin recovery post new facility commissioning
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: FY27 trajectory hinges on July 2026 power transformer facility commissioning as a margin-diversification catalyst, alongside order backlog execution and input cost normalization
